nodes.title: Reject null and primitive root values first
nodes.why: The helper only supports object and array containers, and `null` needs an explicit guard because `typeof null` is `object`.
nodes.alternative: Return primitives unchanged, but that changes this challenge's contract and weakens invalid-input tests.
nodes.tradeoff: Strict validation makes caller mistakes obvious before any clone branch runs.

nodes.title: Clone arrays with `slice()`
nodes.why: `slice()` makes a new top-level array while keeping the function short and familiar.
nodes.tradeoff: Very readable for arrays, but it is still only a shallow copy.

nodes.title: Clone objects with `Object.assign`
nodes.why: `Object.assign({}, value)` creates a new top-level object and copies own enumerable string and symbol properties.
nodes.alternative: Use object spread (`{ ...value }`) for equivalent base semantics in modern JavaScript.
nodes.tradeoff: It ignores inherited prototype properties and does not preserve descriptors, which is exactly the base shallowClone contract here.
